original_hypothesis.title: Solvency failure mode: rewards accrue without enforced funding; early claimers can drain contract balance causing widespread claim outages
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: There is no mechanism ensuring address(this).balance is sufficient to cover accrued rewards, and setRewards can configure arbitrary cantoPerBlock. If funding lags emissions, users’ claim() calls will revert due to failed native transfer. While state will revert and not burn rewardDebt, users experience an operational outage until the contract is funded. Attackers can accelerate depletion by claiming frequently when underfunding exists (capturing available balance ahead of others) without requiring transaction ordering advantages.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if not a code exploit in isolation, this is a high-probability production incident pattern for reward contracts paying from a finite balance with no enforced funding discipline; it becomes an attacker-amplified bank-run dynamic during underfunding events.
deployment_assumptions: Rewards are configured (setRewards) at rates that can exceed or approach the contract’s funded native balance over time
The contract is not continuously topped up to maintain solvency
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment scripts or treasury funding automation provided; receive() exists but no enforced funding logic in contract.

proof_of_signal.pattern: msg.sender.call{value: uint256(cantoToSend)}(""); require(success, "Failed to send CANTO"); // no pre-check of balance; no funding enforcement

preconditions: Contract native balance is less than aggregate pending claimable rewards
At least one user has positive pending rewards in some whitelisted market
Users (or attacker) can call claim() repeatedly to extract remaining balance until depletion

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Rewards are obligations but the contract has no liability accounting or funding guardrails; payouts depend on ad-hoc funding via receive().
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Governance sets emission rates; treasury must fund contract externally.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any underfunded period; attackers can claim as soon as balance becomes available.

operational_questions: Is there an operational process/keeper that tops up the contract proportional to emissions?
What is the maximum daily/weekly emission vs current treasury funding plans?
Are there monitoring/alerts for approaching insolvency (balance vs estimated liabilities)?
validation_requirements: Build an off-chain liability estimator (approximate) from events/state snapshots and compare to balance.
Simulate partial funding and claim behavior to assess how quickly balance is drained and outage duration.
Review treasury ops for replenishment cadence and authorization paths.

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Detect (on-chain) that contract balance is low relative to expected emissions.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Claim frequently across markets/addresses controlled by attacker to capture any topped-up funds immediately after funding transactions land.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Maintain this behavior to starve other users of timely payouts until operations respond.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Claim outages and user trust loss; perceived 'stuck rewards' until funding replenished.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium; requires funding and possibly emission reduction, plus user comms.

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: During underfunding windows; no mempool visibility required.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Frequent claim attempts reverting with Failed to send CANTO; balance approaching zero.
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Treasury top-ups followed immediately by rapid balance depletion.
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: A few addresses claiming at high frequency shortly after top-ups.

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
